DSC_4462.jpg: A Ferrari-shaped safety car in Richard Mille colours leading the Ferrari Challenge pack through a chicane at Le Mans. Full course yellow out, everyone bunched up nose to tail behind it. Do you rate a proper safety car over a virtual one?
DSC_4464.jpg: Silver and blue #22 Ferrari 296 Challenge for idealo, right past the marshal post. Every one of these cars is identical under the skin, so the livery is the only thing telling them apart at speed. Which colour scheme on the grid is your favourite so far?
DSC_4466.jpg: A tight pack of Ferrari Challenge cars snaking through the chicane, black #55 leading the charge. This is what a single-make grid looks like when the racing is close. How many cars do you count nose to tail in this shot?
DSC_4468.jpg: Green camo #9 Ferrari 296 Challenge for Henny's, catching the light past the marshal post. That livery is one of the harder ones to spot mid-pack. Would you go camo or bold colours if it was your car?
DSC_4471.jpg: The #277 Graypaul Ferrari 296 Challenge, e-ppl Group colours, right on the grass line under braking. Every Ferrari Challenge car runs the same 296 underneath the wrap. Do you prefer the subtle liveries or the loud ones?
DSC_4472.jpg: Side profile of the #277 Ferrari 296 Challenge, Ferrari Challenge branding sitting right above the door number. You can see just how identical these chassis are once the wrap comes off in your head. What's the tell you look for to spot one car from another at speed?
DSC_4473.jpg: The Richard Mille safety car pulling the whole Ferrari Challenge field back into line at Le Mans. A medical van sits ready trackside just in case. Do you enjoy safety car periods or do they just kill the race for you?
DSC_4474.jpg: White and red #106 for PlusCity shopping centres, run by Kessel Racing. Gold script down the sill is a nice touch on this one. What's the best sponsor livery you've seen on a Ferrari Challenge car?
DSC_4478.jpg: Teal and black #114 Minax Racing car with an Italian tricolour stripe over the nose. This one stands out even in a sea of red and yellow. Which livery colour combo catches your eye fastest on this grid?
DSC_4479.jpg: Geometric white, red and blue #199 crossing the line at Le Mans. That pattern breaks up the shape of the car in a way plain liveries don't. Do you prefer a clean single colour or a busy pattern like this one?
DSC_4482.jpg: Light blue and orange #130 for Branson Motorsports, leading a camo car and a black Ferrari through the right-hander. Three completely different liveries, same car underneath all three. Which one of these three would you pick to drive?
DSC_4486.jpg: The FIA Medical Car on standby duty at Le Mans, a Porsche Panamera e-hybrid with the blues going. Good to see it out there even when it's not needed for anything serious. Have you ever seen the medical car deployed at a race you were watching?
DSC_4489.jpg: Silver #1 leading the Italian tricolour #4 RPM car and the purple #92 through the esses. A proper bunch of Ferrari Challenge machinery running nose to tail. Which of these three liveries would you want parked in your driveway?
DSC_4490.jpg: Dark purple and orange #44 for Kyokubi, leading a black car and a red Ferrari out of the chicane. The Ferrari Challenge grid never lacks for variety when it comes to paint schemes. What's the story behind your favourite race livery?
DSC_4492.jpg: Purple and yellow-green #92 leading the white idealo car through a tightening right-hander. Every car on this grid is fighting for the same inches of track. Are you backing the loud liveries or the understated ones this season?
DSC_4493.jpg: Dark red #3 for Meverin and Montevertine leading a pair of dark GT cars through the kink. Deep red always reads well under this kind of light. What colour would you never pick for a race car, no matter how good the sponsor deal?
DSC_4494.jpg: The #277 Graypaul Ferrari running wide onto the grass line, e-ppl Group colours catching the low sun. Every inch of track gets used in a race like this. Do you think running wide like this costs more time than it saves?
DSC_4495.jpg: Red #155 Golimo car leading a zebra-striped Ram entry and a blue car through the chicane. That zebra livery has to be one of the more unusual wraps on the grid. What's the strangest race livery you've ever seen?
DSC_4497.jpg: Yellow-green Ferrari leading a red car past a pink and tricolour-liveried entry. Marshals watching it all unfold from the fence line above. Which corner of a circuit gives you the best view as a spectator?
DSC_4498.jpg: Light blue, pink and black #306 with an Italian tricolour stripe, leading through the right-hander. A purple car and a pale blue car chase close behind. How do you rate this year's Ferrari Challenge grid for livery variety?
DSC_4499.jpg: Purple and yellow #321 leading the tricolour-striped #169 through the bend. Light blue and orange machinery still visible further back in the pack. Are you following the Ferrari Challenge championship this season?
DSC_4502.jpg: Close up on #130 Branson Motorsports, Race for RP charity branding right there on the door. Good to see a charity cause getting proper visibility on the side of a race car. Have you ever raced or sponsored a car for charity?
DSC_4507.jpg: Kessel Racing's #18 leading its sister #74, both in matching red and yellow. Team liveries running together always look sharp on track. Do you prefer matching team colours or every car getting its own identity?
DSC_4508.jpg: Grey #1 with a red and yellow stripe, chasing a red Ferrari through the fast right-hander. Simple liveries like this one age better than most. What's a livery you thought was boring at first but grew on you?
DSC_4515.jpg: Italian tricolour #198 running side by side with Kessel Racing's black and orange #186 for Benzoil. Proper door to door racing right here. Who do you reckon comes out ahead in this battle?
DSC_4516.jpg: Blue and yellow #175 for Seafood C'Z, past the start line markers. Not every sponsor on this grid is a household name, and that's part of the charm. What's the most unusual sponsor you've spotted on a race car?
DSC_4518.jpg: White, red and blue #199 leading a black Ferrari through the esses, a zebra car and a pink one further back. This grid never runs short of colour. Which of these liveries would you pick out from the crowd first?
DSC_4519.jpg: White, red and blue #180 for Gohm leading the blue and black #113 past a yellow-green car. Every one of these Ferraris is fighting for track position. Are you drawn to the front runners or the midfield battles more?
DSC_4520.jpg: Yellow #369 for Japanese retailer Car Guy, tricolour stripe over the nose, crossing the finish straight. Sponsorship on this grid really does span the globe. What's the furthest-travelled sponsor you've noticed at a European race?
DSC_4522.jpg: Red and light blue #348 exiting past the start-finish kerbing. Clean, simple, effective livery on this one. Do you prefer your race cars loud or understated?
DSC_4534.jpg: White, red and blue #180 for Gohm leaning hard through a fast right-hander. Same car, different corner, still finding the limit. What's your favourite type of corner to watch cars attack?
DSC_4535.jpg: Lime green and red #150 for MEO Arena, crossing the line with a purple car trailing behind. That green cuts through everything else on the grid. Would you ever go full neon on a race liveries of your own?
DSC_4537.jpg: Black and gold #181 for The Moon Group, shark-teeth nose leading a peach car and a yellow car into the hairpin. That nose design is properly menacing up close. What's the most aggressive livery design you've seen on a race car?
DSC_4539.jpg: Black and yellow #102 for MR Solar and Stern, running past the Raceborn hoarding. A teal car chases on the straight behind. Solar sponsorship on a petrol-powered race car is a nice bit of irony. Do you think we'll see more crossover sponsors like this?
DSC_4543.jpg: Head on with #130 Branson Motorsports, light blue and orange, hard on the kerbing. Face on shots like this really show off the aero on these cars. What's your favourite angle to shoot a race car from?
DSC_4544.jpg: The #130 Branson Motorsports car running past a packed grandstand hill. Race for RP charity branding getting a great backdrop here. Have you ever picked your seat at a race just for the view like this?
20210905_151041.jpg: Sea of orange at Zandvoort, and a Ferrari splitting right through the middle of it. This was F1's first trip back to the Dutch Grand Prix in 36 years, and the atmosphere was something else. Have you ever been to a home race for a driver as popular as Verstappen?
20210905_151048.jpg: Two cars, one enormous orange crowd. Zandvoort's grandstands were rammed for the return of the Dutch Grand Prix in 2021. What's the loudest a crowd has ever been for a race you've watched?
20210905_151256b.jpg: Home advantage doesn't get much bigger than this. The Red Bull running down towards Tarzan with an entire grandstand of orange behind it. Are you Team Orange or does another driver have your loyalty?
20210905_151256.jpg: Leading the pack at Verstappen's home race. There's nothing quite like the noise a Dutch crowd makes when their car is out front. Do you reckon home races add real pace or is it all in the driver's head?
20210905_160317.jpg: That banked final corner at Zandvoort is unlike anything else on the calendar. Watching an Aston Martin lean into it from up close was proper special. Which circuit feature would you love to see more tracks copy?
20210905_160320.jpg: Ferrari, flat out through Zandvoort's banked final corner. The banking here lets these cars carry so much more speed than the layout has any right to allow. What's your favourite banked corner in motorsport?
20210905_160338.jpg: Red Bull holding off the chasing Alpine through Zandvoort's banked final corner. This corner rewards commitment more than almost anywhere else on the calendar. Would you have the nerve to carry full speed through a banked corner like this?
20210905_122456.jpg: Before the main event, the Porsche support race warming the crowd up nicely. Same banked corner, same orange wall of fans, just a different set of cars. Do you rate a good support race as much as the headline act?
20210905_122659.jpg: Four identical Porsche 911 GT3 Cup cars, nose to tail through Zandvoort's banking. In a one-make series like this it really comes down to who commits hardest into that corner. Do you prefer watching a single-make grid or one with more variety?
20210905_122700.jpg: Same corner, same cars, different angle. The Porsche support race made full use of that banking all morning. What's your favourite one-make racing series to watch?
DSC_5117.jpg: Fiesta Zetec racing in the wet at Mondello Park. The #42 AV1 Tiles & Bathrooms car and the #65 Wrightz car throwing spray everywhere. Do you prefer watching wet races or dry ones?
DSC_5134.jpg: Hesketh tribute liveries never get old. This black and gold single-seater carries a Brands Hatch Driver of the Day sticker and Ash Law's name on the cockpit, spotted in the Mondello Park paddock. What's your favourite tribute livery in motorsport?
DSC_5136.jpg: Sports 2000 machinery in the Mondello Park paddock. Clive Hayes' #12 car sitting quietly before its next outing. Have you ever watched a Sports 2000 race up close?
DSC_5137.jpg: Purple and white, nose in, engine hanging out the back. Sports prototypes like this one turn the paddock into as much of a show as the track. What do you make of rear-engined club racers?
DSC_5139.jpg: Formula Vee parked up next to a teal prototype at Mondello Park. Different classes, same paddock, same obsession. Which class would you follow if you had to pick just one?
DSC_5140.jpg: Blue and yellow prototype waiting its turn at Mondello Park. That Oulton Park Driver of the Day sticker says plenty about this car's history. Do you read every sticker on a race car when you get close?
DSC_5141.jpg: Andy Keogh's Red Bull helmet resting in the cockpit of his #83 Formula Vee. TyreDoctor.ie backing the car for another round at Mondello Park. What's the best helmet design you've seen on the grid this year?
DSC_5143.jpg: Roger Donnan's #34 Sports 2000 in green, black and red at Mondello Park. MCR Race Cars on the nose, ready for another outing. Which Sports 2000 livery catches your eye the most?
DSC_5146.jpg: Blue and grey #28 Sports 2000, nose to nose with #34 in the paddock. Two very different liveries, same class, same Sunday. Do you prefer bold liveries or something more understated?
DSC_5151.jpg: The Wrap Pack Fiesta, purple and green graffiti wrapped over black. Fuelled by feminine rage, according to the rear glass. What's the boldest wrap you've seen on a race car?
DSC_5152.jpg: Fiesta ST Championship car, black with blue accents, backed by Marmox and BuildWright. Rear-on in the Mondello Park paddock before the next race. Fiesta ST or Fiesta Zetec, which class would you rather race?
DSC_5156.jpg: Stephen Roche's #17 green Formula Vee, Mulranny Park Hotel on the flank. Lined up and ready at Mondello Park. Would you have the patience for a single-make series like Formula Vee?
DSC_5157.jpg: ZK Auto Supplies Mini, #14, turning in at Mondello Park. Junior Mini Challenge racing is closer than most people expect. Did you know Ireland had its own Mini race series?
DSC_5158.jpg: Three Minis, one corner, #99 leading the charge at Mondello Park. Junior Mini Challenge racing packs a proper punch for such small cars. Which of these three liveries is your favourite?
DSC_5165.jpg: Blue #6 leading black and green #97 through a Mondello Park corner. Junior Mini Challenge racing, tight as ever. Do small, evenly matched cars make for the best racing?
DSC_5166.jpg: Shot low through the grass, #14 charging past at Mondello Park. Sometimes the best angle is the one closest to the ground. What's your favourite low angle shot you've taken?
DSC_5168.jpg: #16 leads #65 and #73 through a Mondello Park corner. Junior Mini Challenge grids are stacked deep this year. How many cars do you reckon are fighting for position in this shot?
DSC_5171.jpg: Dun Laoghaire Motor Worx orange and white Mini, #77, carrying the Mondello Park badge itself through the corner. Nice touch having the circuit's own logo on the car. Have you ever spotted a circuit's own branding on a race car?
DSC_5173.jpg: Blue and green camo Mini, #11, turning in hard at Mondello Park. Junior Mini Challenge cars punch above their size. What's your take on camo liveries on a race car?
DSC_5174.jpg: #11 running wide, dust flying at Mondello Park. Junior Mini Challenge racing doesn't get any less committed off the racing line. Ever caught a car running wide like this on camera?
DSC_5187.jpg: Heapes' #9 black and red Mini, Hillside Autos and Iconic Auto-Design on the flanks. Mondello Park corner, Junior Mini Challenge in full swing. Which sponsor livery on this grid is your favourite?
DSC_5189.jpg: #11 threading past the tyre wall at Mondello Park. Junior Mini Challenge racing leaves very little room for error. How close do you like your racing shots to the action?
DSC_5194.jpg: Heapes' #9 leading a blue and a yellow Mini past the tyre wall at Mondello Park. Junior Mini Challenge racing, three wide and no room to spare. Which of these three would you back to win?
DSC_5197.jpg: #20 DDE-liveried Mini leading #16 through a Mondello Park corner. Junior Mini Challenge racing stays tight lap after lap. Red and white or silver and red, which livery wins it for you?
DSC_5198.jpg: HD Dennis Leasing's #16 Mini turning in at Mondello Park. Junior Mini Challenge cars might be small, but they don't hang about. What's the appeal of racing something this compact?
DSC_5202.jpg: A Toyota Celica GT-Four, #32 for Hawk X Motorsport, parked next to a yellow and black saloon with a Catch Me James, For Sale sticker. Paddock humour never gets old at Mondello Park. What's the funniest sticker you've spotted on a race car?
DSC_5207.jpg: Pink and black camo Formula Vee, engine and gearbox laid bare at the back. These cars hide nothing once the bodywork's off. Do you prefer seeing the mechanical side of a race car or keeping the mystery?
DSC_5213.jpg: #78 red Formula Vee leads Stephen Roche's #17 green car through a Mondello Park corner. Formula Vee racing stays close all the way through the field. Which colour combo on this grid stands out most to you?
DSC_5215.jpg: #15 orange Formula Vee sliding wide past the tyre wall at Mondello Park. These cars might be simple, but they're still a handful at the limit. Have you ever driven a car with this little aero help?
DSC_5217.jpg: Harry's #80 blue Formula Vee, Blanco Nino and VWSpares.ie on the flanks. Turning in hard at Mondello Park. What's your favourite Formula Vee livery from this grid so far?
DSC_5221.jpg: #22 leading a green Formula Vee past the Mondello Park grandstand. Nothing beats seeing the circuit's own signage in the background of a good battle. Do you prefer shots with the venue clearly in frame?
DSC_5223.jpg: #95 red, white and blue Formula Vee cutting through a corner in front of the Mondello Park grandstand. Simple, quick, and relentless, that's Formula Vee racing. Would you rather start out in Formula Vee or go straight to something bigger?
DSC_5228.jpg: #78 red Formula Vee running wheel to wheel with a red, white and blue rival at Mondello Park. This is what close racing looks like in a spec class. How many corners do you reckon this battle lasted?
DSC_5229.jpg: Unpainted grey and black Formula Vee at Mondello Park, carbon-effect bodywork left completely bare. Sometimes the rawest looking cars are the most honest ones. Would you leave a race car unpainted if it was yours?
DSC_5231.jpg: #7 white and orange Formula Vee cutting through a Mondello Park corner. Formula Vee has been producing sharp racing for decades and it still delivers. What's your pick for the best-looking Formula Vee on this grid?
DSC_5232.jpg: #88 pink and black camo Formula Vee turning in at Mondello Park. Loud livery, quiet class, in the best way. Pink or classic team colours, which do you prefer on a race car?
DSC_5233.jpg: #56 Formula Vee in full Martini stripes, backed by Selco and SM Racing. That livery works on absolutely anything. Martini stripes, timeless or overdone at this point?

DSC_5496.jpg: Roger Donnan's #34 Sports 2000 cutting hard through a Mondello Park corner. MCR Race Cars and Ancaster backing the green machine. Do you follow Sports 2000 racing much?
DSC_5498.jpg: Blue #62 Sports 2000, JLE Systems Ltd on the flanks, turning in at Mondello Park. These cars might be small but they carry serious pace through the corners. What's your take on Sports 2000 as a class?
DSC_5500.jpg: Orange, black and blue #7 Sports 2000 cutting through a Mondello Park corner. Varley Red Top backing this one. Which livery on this Sports 2000 grid is your favourite so far?
DSC_5502.jpg: Teal #25 Sports 2000 turning in hard at Mondello Park. Simple, light, and quick, that's the whole appeal of this class. Would you rather race something minimal like this or a heavier tin-top?
DSC_5505.jpg: Ash Law sending #73 sideways with the tyres lit up at Mondello Park. Creative backing the black and gold car. Do you prefer watching a car on the edge like this or a clean, tidy lap?
DSC_5509.jpg: Lycian Events Motorsport's #24 Sports 2000, Just Tickets on the nose, turning in at Mondello Park. What's your favourite sponsor livery from this Sports 2000 grid?
DSC_5526.jpg: Richard Johnson's #21 Sports 2000, black, red and white for Ancaster Leisure Motorsport, cutting through a Mondello Park corner. Would you have the nerve to race wheel to wheel in one of these?
DSC_5528.jpg: MCR Race Cars' #8 Sports 2000, black and purple, turning in at Mondello Park. Hoosier rubber doing the work through the corner. What's the most striking colour scheme you've seen on a Sports 2000 car?
DSC_5530.jpg: Musical notes and flames down the side of this #82 Sports 2000 at Mondello Park. Someone had fun designing this livery. What's the most creative livery you've ever seen on a race car?
DSC_5555.jpg: Two Sports 2000 cars, nose to tail, seen from behind at Mondello Park. The #62 blue car chasing hard. Do you prefer shooting cars head on or from behind like this?
DSC_5577.jpg: Williams in the #24 Lycian Events Sports 2000, Le Mans Art now on the flanks, cutting hard at Mondello Park. What's your favourite thing about watching a spec class like this up close?
DSC_5580.jpg: Roger Donnan's #34 chasing the #88 YTCC-liveried Sports 2000 through a Mondello Park corner. Blue and yellow against green, black and red. Which of these two liveries wins it for you?
DSC_5582.jpg: Sports 2000 cars running under Mondello Park's Alfa Romeo bridge. #62 leading #7 through one of the more recognisable spots on the circuit. What's your favourite bridge or landmark corner at any track?
DSC_5592.jpg: Blue, white and yellow #33 Sports 2000 turning in at Mondello Park. Every one of these cars looks completely different once you get up close. Do you prefer bold liveries or something more understated?
DSC_5594.jpg: #33 Sports 2000 running past the Nordic Spirit and Campion boards at Mondello Park. Trackside advertising is its own bit of history at every circuit. Do you ever notice the sponsor boards as much as the cars?
DSC_5595.jpg: Three Formula Sheane cars nose to tail at Mondello Park, #18 Kiernan Autos leading the way. Castrol boards lining the barrier behind. Which of these three would you back to come out on top?
DSC_5597.jpg: #18, #15 and #35 running three wide into a Mondello Park corner. Formula Sheane racing doesn't leave much room to spare. How many corners do you reckon this battle lasted?
DSC_5609.jpg: Kevin Igoe's #5 Formula Sheane, Michael Jury Carpet Fitting Services and Irish Flooring Products on the flanks, at Mondello Park. Proper grassroots sponsorship on this one. What's the best local sponsor livery you've spotted at a track?
DSC_5613.jpg: #27 Formula Sheane wearing a tribute to the classic McLaren Marlboro livery at Mondello Park. That colour scheme works on absolutely anything. Tribute liveries, yes or no?
DSC_5614.jpg: Spotted in the car park at Mondello Park, a silver E46 M3 with quad exhausts and staggered alloys. Sometimes the car park is as good as the paddock. What's the best car you've ever spotted in a track car park?
DSC_5622.jpg: Gulf-style #35 Formula Sheane exiting the pit lane area at Mondello Park. That light blue and orange combo never gets old. Would you rather see Gulf colours on a Formula car or a GT car?
DSC_5623.jpg: Garrett's #15 Formula Sheane, Global Home Care backing the white and blue car, at Mondello Park. What's your favourite Formula Sheane livery from this grid?
DSC_5624.jpg: #7 Formula Sheane running past the paddock at Mondello Park, a SILTEX Fiesta Zetec car parked up behind. Multiple classes, one meeting, that's club racing at its best. Which class would you want to try first?
DSC_5625.jpg: #27 Formula Sheane turning in at Mondello Park, that Marlboro tribute livery working every angle. What's your favourite historic livery that's been brought back to life on a modern car?
DSC_5629.jpg: #15 and #7 running through a Mondello Park corner, a circuit-liveried van parked up in the paddock behind. Formula Sheane racing stays close all the way through the grid. Which of these two would you back?
